title: Forecasting the Research Frontier — pre-registered NeurIPS 2026 predictions
method: 9 parallel research scouts over public Feb-Jun 2026 signals (arXiv, ICLR 2026, ICML 2026); synthesis; 3 adversarial critics; share-not-count discipline; every claim carries a falsifiable December criterion.
note: This file is the immutable record of the forecast. Its SHA-256 is published on research.upneja.ai; the public git commit that first locked these ten (2026-06-21) is the timestamp. Editing any prediction below would change the hash and break the pre-registration.

predictions.title: Evaluation becomes a science: sandbagging, eval-awareness, and benchmark auditing
predictions.criterion: The Evaluations & Datasets track ships, AND >=20 accepted papers' primary contribution is auditing existing benchmarks / contamination detection / eval-awareness / construct validity, AND >=1 gets a spotlight, oral, or award.

predictions.title: RLVR escapes verifiable domains via rubric and generative verifiers; GRPO-successors become the default
predictions.criterion: >=80 accepted papers carry RLVR / GRPO / verifiable-reward (or a named successor) in title/abstract, AND >=5 center rubric/generative verifiers for non-verifiable domains, AND an RLVR/reasoning-RL paper wins an oral/award.

predictions.title: Agentic RL where the environment, not the algorithm, is the scarce asset
predictions.criterion: >=40 accepted papers train LLM agents end-to-end with RL in interactive environments, >=6 foreground long-horizon credit assignment, AND >=1 agentic-RL oral/spotlight or a dedicated workshop.

predictions.title: World models as trainable simulators, with physics-faithfulness as the open problem
predictions.criterion: world model in >=50 accepted titles/abstracts with >=10 using it as a trainable simulator, AND >=1 spotlight/oral or a World Models workshop, AND >=3 papers on physics-faithfulness/controllability.

predictions.title: Unified, reasoning-infused multimodal: one backbone that reasons, then renders
predictions.criterion: Multimodal/VLM is the largest single topic cluster among accepted papers, AND >=15 papers center unified understanding-and-generation or reasoning-infused image generation, AND >=1 spotlight/oral or dedicated workshop.

predictions.title: Diffusion language models become a recognized alternative to autoregressive text
predictions.criterion: >=20 accepted main-proceedings papers on diffusion/masked-diffusion language models, AND (>=1 diffusion-LM oral/spotlight OR a dedicated workshop/tutorial).

predictions.title: Hybrid linear-attention beats pure SSM; attention is contested again
predictions.criterion: >=30 accepted papers on hybrid/linear-attention/sub-quadratic architectures, AND >=2 of {Mamba-3, Kimi Linear, Olmo Hybrid, Gated DeltaNet-2, Qwen3-Next} as named baselines, AND hybrids outnumber pure-SSM among new-backbone papers.

predictions.title: Interpretability after SAEs: transcoders, circuit tracing, parameter decomposition
predictions.criterion: >=10 accepted papers center post-SAE interpretability (transcoders/CLTs/model-diffing/parameter-decomposition), SAE-replacement explicit in >=2, AND >=1 interpretability spotlight/oral or named mech-interp workshop.

predictions.title: AI for mathematics: autonomous theorem proving cracks open problems
predictions.criterion: >=1 newly machine-verified mathematics result presented as an oral/keynote/invited talk, OR an AI-for-math workshop runs, AND FrontierMath/Lean-prover benchmarks cited across >=15 accepted papers.

predictions.title: Test-time training: updating weights during inference
predictions.criterion: >=12 accepted main-proceedings papers center test-time training/weight-adaptation/continual-at-inference for sequence models or LLMs (excluding classic vision TTA), AND >=1 spotlight/oral or a TTT-adjacent workshop.

watchlist.title: Inference-aware scaling laws & the efficiency inversion
The science of peer review under AI load
